perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, the COMPANY will not be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction. However, COMSYS will be legally obligated to continue negotiations in good faith, even without a definitive agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 5. Originally, both parties were free from legal obligation until a definitive agreement. Now, the modified text states that COMPANY is free from obligation, but COMSYS is obligated to continue negotiations. This directly contradicts the original intent of both parties being on equal footing until a final agreement is reached and creates an in-text contradiction. This change impacts the obligations of both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, the COMPANY will not be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction. However, COMSYS will be legally obligated to continue negotiations in good faith, even without a definitive agreement. In addition, neither Party will be under any obligation to negotiate a definitive agreement, by virtue of this or any written or oral expression, discussion or negotiation with respect to such Transaction by any or either of the Representatives of the Parties except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. 2
perturbation.justification: The original text states that both COMPANY and COMSYS are not under any legal obligation until a definitive agreement is reached. The changed text modifies this, stating that COMPANY is not under legal obligation, but COMSYS is legally obligated to continue negotiations even without a definitive agreement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning it also holds this contradictory statement. This directly contradicts the original intent of both parties being on equal footing, creating a clear and direct conflict within the document regarding each party's obligations, fitting the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY agrees that, for a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, unless COMPANY shall have been specifically invited in writing by COMSYS, neither COMPANY nor its Representatives as advisor to COMPANY as principal will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Act of 1934) with respect to the securities of COMSYS; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence management or the board of directors or policies of COMSYS; (d) take any action which might force COMSYS to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing; provided , however , that if COMSYS makes a public announcement that it has entered into a definitive agreement that contemplates a business combination transaction between COMSYS and a third party that would result in a change of control of COMSYS, or in the event any third party has made a tender offer for a majority of the then outstanding securities of COMSYS entitled to be voted generally in the election of directors, then COMPANY may make a proposal to the board of directors of COMSYS with respect to a business combination transaction involving COMPANY and COMSYS.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY agrees that, for a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, unless COMPANY shall have been specifically invited in writing by COMSYS, neither COMPANY nor its Representatives as advisor to COMPANY as principal will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Act of 1934) with respect to the securities of COMSYS; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence management or the board of directors or policies of COMSYS; (d) take any action which might force COMSYS to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this restriction shall not apply if COMSYS's stock price falls below $5 per share for 30 consecutive days.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 8 states the conditions under which COMPANY is allowed to make a proposal to the board of directors of COMSYS with respect to a business combination. It allows COMPANY to make a proposal if COMSYS makes a public announcement of a business combination with a third party or if a third party makes a tender offer for a majority of COMSYS's securities. The modified text adds a new condition: the restriction does not apply if COMSYS's stock price falls below $5 per share for 30 consecutive days. This creates a contradiction as it introduces a new scenario where COMPANY's restrictions are lifted, changing the original conditions outlined in the agreement. However, the most directly impacted section remains Section 8 itself, where these conditions are defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY agrees that, for a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, unless COMPANY shall have been specifically invited in writing by COMSYS, neither COMPANY nor its Representatives as advisor to COMPANY as principal will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Act of 1934) with respect to the securities of COMSYS; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence management or the board of directors or policies of COMSYS; (d) take any action which might force COMSYS to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this restriction shall not apply if COMSYS's stock price falls below $5 per share for 30 consecutive days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new condition where COMPANY's restrictions are lifted if COMSYS's stock price falls below $5 per share for 30 consecutive days, which directly contradicts the original conditions outlined in the contradicted text (which is the same section). This inconsistency regarding the circumstances under which COMPANY is restricted from certain actions constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, because the original text did not have that clause, and now they both contradict each other.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the Parties and supersedes any and all other agreements, if any, between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the Parties with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the Parties, except for any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this document, which shall also be considered binding. This Agreement supersedes any and all other written agreements, if any, between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the Parties with respect thereto.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 11. The original text asserts that the written agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement, superseding all other agreements. The modified text contradicts this by stating that oral agreements made prior to signing are also binding, creating an in-text contradiction. This undermines the entire purpose of the integration clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the Parties, except for any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this document, which shall also be considered binding. This Agreement supersedes any and all other written agreements, if any, between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the Parties with respect thereto. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes all prior collateral communications, if any, regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the Parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed. No modification or amendment of this Agreement and no waiver of the terms and conditions hereof, in whole or in part, shall be binding, except by a written agreement signed by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts the written agreement is the complete agreement, superseding all others. The changed text contradicts this by stating oral agreements are also binding. The contradicted text also mentions that the agreement supersedes all prior communications and can only be amended in writing. This creates a direct contradiction, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: No modification or amendment of this Agreement and no waiver of the terms and conditions hereof, in whole or in part, shall be binding, except by a written agreement signed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: No modification or amendment of this Agreement and no waiver of the terms and conditions hereof, in whole or in part, shall be binding, except by a written agreement signed by the Parties, or by documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 11. The original text strictly requires written agreements signed by both parties for any modifications, amendments, or waivers. The modification allows email correspondence to also serve as binding agreements for such changes. This directly contradicts the original requirement for formal, signed written agreements. This change impacts the enforceability of future alterations to the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the Parties, except for any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this document, which shall also be considered binding. This Agreement supersedes any and all other written agreements, if any, between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the Parties with respect thereto. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes all prior collateral communications, if any, regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the Parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed. No modification or amendment of this Agreement and no waiver of the terms and conditions hereof, in whole or in part, shall be binding, except by a written agreement signed by the Parties, or by documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by allowing email correspondence to serve as binding agreements for modifications, amendments, or waivers. The contradicted text states that 'No modification or amendment of this Agreement and no waiver of the terms and conditions hereof, in whole or in part, shall be binding, except by a written agreement signed by the Parties'. The changed text directly contradicts this by adding 'or by documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties', thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraph 8 above, this Agreement and all obligations of the parties hereunder, notwithstanding anything to the contrary that may be contained herein, shall terminate one (1) year from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraph 8 above, this Agreement and all obligations of the parties hereunder, notwithstanding anything to the contrary that may be contained herein, shall terminate one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, except that the confidentiality obligations outlined in paragraph 1 shall remain in effect for five (5) years.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a qualifier to Section 14. The original text specifies that the agreement and all obligations terminate one year from the agreement date, with an exception for Paragraph 8. The added text creates an exception for Paragraph 1, the confidentiality obligations, stating that they stay in place for five years. This creates an in-text contradiction, since the original agreement had a blanket termination, now one section is indefinite. This change impacts the obligations of both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraph 8 above, this Agreement and all obligations of the parties hereunder, notwithstanding anything to the contrary that may be contained herein, shall terminate one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, except that the confidentiality obligations outlined in paragraph 1 shall remain in effect for five (5) years. 4 Manpower Inc., a Wisconsin corporation Comsys IT Partners, Inc., a Delaware corporation By: /s/ Michael J. Lynch Name: Michael J. Lynch By: /s/ David L. Kerr Name: David L. Kerr Vice President, General Counsel International Senior Vice President Title Title November 19, 2009 11/19/09 Date Date 5
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 14, which originally stipulated that the entire agreement, including all obligations, terminates one year from the agreement date, except for Paragraph 8. The changed text creates an exception for Paragraph 1, specifying that the confidentiality obligations remain in effect for five years. This introduces a direct contradiction because the agreement now has a blanket termination with a specific section that is not terminated, leading to conflicting enforcement of obligations, creating an inconsistency as defined in the Perturbation definition.
